title: QueryForge
description: A complete relational database engine — lexer, Pratt parser, a statistics-driven cost-based optimizer with cost-based join reordering (Selinger-style subset DP), index-only (covering) scans, bitmap-AND of multiple indexes and composite B+Tree indexes, a Volcano-model executor with hash & sort-merge joins, an external merge sort, GROUPING SETS/ROLLUP/CUBE with GROUPING(), ordered-set aggregates (PERCENTILE_CONT/DISC, MODE), correlated subqueries, CTEs (incl. WITH RECURSIVE), set operations, window functions with explicit ROWS/RANGE frames, statistical aggregates with FILTER, transactions, CSV import, an SVG chart view and an EXPLAIN visualizer. First-class temporal types — DATE, TIME, TIMESTAMP and INTERVAL as tagged values that index, sort, group and join like any column — with typed literals, calendar-aware date arithmetic (month-clamping), EXTRACT/DATE_TRUNC/AGE, MAKE_* constructors and CURRENT_DATE/TIMESTAMP. Full declarative integrity: PRIMARY KEY/UNIQUE (composite), CHECK and DEFAULT, and FOREIGN KEYs with ON DELETE/UPDATE referential actions (CASCADE/RESTRICT/SET NULL/SET DEFAULT, recursively), ALTER TABLE schema evolution, and atomic statements that roll back wholesale on any constraint failure. And first-class exact numerics: arbitrary-precision DECIMAL/NUMERIC (BigInt-backed) where 0.1 + 0.2 is exactly 0.3 and money SUMs never lose a cent — DECIMAL(p,s) columns, exact arithmetic & aggregates, and Postgres-style TO_CHAR currency formatting. Built from scratch in TypeScript, running entirely in the browser.
